purpose: Agent-only routing and capability catalog for MCP server selection. Use this file to choose the right MCP surface before using skills, shell tools, or broader fallbacks.

machine_catalog.purpose: Full machine-readable MCP inventory and routing rules.

selection_rules: Prefer apple-docs for Apple framework and API questions.
Prefer context7 for current non-Apple library and framework docs.
Prefer fff for file discovery before broad manual repo crawling.
Prefer xcodebuildmcp for Apple build, run, test, simulator, log, and automation workflows.
Prefer xcode only for Xcode-directed IDE actions.
Prefer node_repl when persistent JavaScript execution or JS-based automation loops are needed.
Prefer specialized MCPs over generic execution surfaces.

task_routes.task: Apple API docs, framework semantics, and platform availability
Current non-Apple framework, SDK, or library docs
Find relevant files or modules in a repo
Build, run, test, inspect, or automate an Apple project
Ask Xcode to perform an IDE-specific action
Automate macOS host or app behavior
Use installed app or connector-backed capabilities

mcps.name: apple-docs
mcps.use_when: Apple API docs, platform availability, and official Apple framework references.
mcps.avoid_when: Non-Apple library docs are the primary need.

mcps.name: codex_apps
mcps.use_when: Installed apps and connector-backed tools exposed through Codex apps.
mcps.avoid_when: A direct MCP already covers the task more narrowly.

mcps.name: context7
mcps.use_when: Current non-Apple library and framework documentation, migration notes, setup guidance, and API references.

mcps.name: excalidraw
mcps.avoid_when: The task is code execution or documentation lookup.

mcps.name: fff
mcps.use_when: Fast file finding and project-local file exploration.
mcps.avoid_when: The task is not about repo structure or file discovery.

mcps.name: idesign
mcps.use_when: Design and visual-generation oriented workflows when it is enabled in the target machine's config.
mcps.avoid_when: The current config snapshot disables it, or a more specific design MCP such as pencil or excalidraw is clearly a better fit.

mcps.name: ifallow
mcps.use_when: Policy-aware gating and allowlist-style tool workflows.
mcps.avoid_when: The task does not depend on policy enforcement behavior.

mcps.name: macpilot
mcps.use_when: macOS automation and host interaction workflows.
mcps.avoid_when: Repo-local tools already cover the task without host control.

mcps.name: node_repl
mcps.use_when: Persistent JavaScript execution, browser control helpers, and scripted automation.
mcps.avoid_when: A specialized MCP directly solves the task.

mcps.name: pencil
mcps.avoid_when: The task does not involve Pencil design files.

mcps.name: xcode
mcps.use_when: Request Xcode to perform Apple-platform IDE actions.
mcps.avoid_when: The task is better served by xcodebuildmcp workflow tools.

mcps.name: xcodebuildmcp
mcps.use_when: Discover, build, run, test, inspect, and automate Apple projects and simulators.
mcps.avoid_when: The task is only a docs lookup.
